measured_vs_derived.KD_nM: measured (SPR, monovalent Fab vs wild-type human 4-1BB)
measured_vs_derived.kon_perM_pers: not extracted (SPR kinetics in Suppl. Fig 8 of source; numeric kon not in text)

sources.KD_nM: Chin SM et al., Nat Commun 2018;9:4679. DOI 10.1038/s41467-018-07136-7; PMID 30410017. Full text retrieved and read: 'Our SPR experiments measuring monovalent binding of utomilumab and urelumab to wild type h4-1BB give KDs of 69 and 22 nM, respectively (Supplementary Figure 8).'
sources.kint_perday: class-estimate: no molecule-specific internalization rate published for this costim receptor + agonist mAb; order-of-magnitude default for TNFRSF/CD28-family costim receptors (between CD20~0.02 and HER2~0.17/day)
sources.kdeg_perday: class-estimate: no molecule-specific free-receptor turnover published; default for inducible costim receptor (t1/2 ~1.4 day)
sources.mw_kda: class-estimate: full-length IgG standard MW 145-150 kDa (no molecule-specific sequence MW retrieved)
sources.half_life_days: BMS Clinical Protocol CA186107 (NCT02253992), PDF retrieved: 'half-life of urelumab in subjects with solid malignancies was approximately 125-135 hours (5.2-5.6 days).' Midpoint 5.4 d; nonlinear/TMDD at low doses.

urelumab.notes: Current placeholder KD=2.2e-08 M (22 nM) CONFIRMED correct - not changed. Urelumab (BMS-663513) binds 4-1BB CRD-1, away from ligand site (non-ligand-blocking). IgG4. PK is nonlinear/target-mediated; reported t1/2 5.2-5.6 d is at low doses (0.1-0.3 mg/kg) and increases with dose. kon/koff exist in source Suppl. Fig 8 but numeric values not extractable here.

measured_vs_derived.KD_nM: measured (binding affinity, primary source retrieved; site distinct from CD40L)

sources.KD_nM: Gladue RP et al., Cancer Immunol Immunother 2011;60(7):1009-1017. DOI 10.1007/s00262-011-1014-6. Full text retrieved (PMC): 'CP-870,893 is a fully human, IgG2 antibody that selectively interacts with CD40 at a site distinct from its ligand-binding region with a KD of 0.4 nM.' Corroborated by Li DK & Wang W review (PMC7471753, retrieved): 'CP-870,893 ... binds CD40 with a very high affinity (Kd of 0.4 nmol/l).'
sources.kint_perday: class-estimate: no molecule-specific internalization rate published for this costim receptor + agonist mAb; order-of-magnitude default for TNFRSF/CD28-family costim receptors (between CD20~0.02 and HER2~0.17/day)
sources.kdeg_perday: class-estimate: no molecule-specific free-receptor turnover published; default for inducible costim receptor (t1/2 ~1.4 day)
sources.mw_kda: class-estimate: full-length IgG standard MW 145-150 kDa (no molecule-specific sequence MW retrieved)
sources.route: Roche/clinical (RO7009789); Rüter J et al. Cancer Biol Ther 2010;10:983-993 (IV); MTD ~0.2 mg/kg
sources.half_life_days: no clean numeric terminal t1/2 found in accessible sources; clinical PK shows rapid clearance (bell-shaped/TMDD); MTD ~0.2 mg/kg IV weekly

selicrelumab.notes: Placeholder KD=1.0 REPLACED with 0.4 nM (Gladue 2011). Selicrelumab = CP-870,893 / RG-7876 / RO7009789, fully human IgG2, agonism crosslink-independent, does NOT block CD40L. Half-life left null (no citable numeric value; known short/target-mediated). Also studied SC.

measured_vs_derived.KD_nM: measured-per-secondary-review (0.01 nM from retrieved review PMC7471753; primary SPR paper Mangsbo 2015 paywalled/not directly retrievable this session)
measured_vs_derived.kon_perM_pers: not extracted (qualitatively 'very fast on-rate'; numeric value in Suppl. Table S2)

sources.KD_nM: Value 0.01 nM (10 pM) cited from Li DK & Wang W, 'Characteristics and clinical trial results of agonistic anti-CD40 antibodies in the treatment of malignancies' (PMC7471753), full text retrieved: 'ADC-1013 ... is a fully human agonistic anti-CD40 IgG1 mAb with high affinity for CD40 (Kd=0.01 nM).' PRIMARY source is the affinity-maturation paper Mangsbo SM et al., Clin Cancer Res 2015;21(5):1115-1126 (DOI 10.1158/1078-0432.CCR-14-0913; PMID 25316820) but its full text is paywalled and could NOT be retrieved in-session to verify the exact figure directly; secondary review used instead. Deronic 2021 (retrieved) discusses mitazalimab but reports no KD.
sources.kint_perday: class-estimate: no molecule-specific internalization rate published for this costim receptor + agonist mAb; order-of-magnitude default for TNFRSF/CD28-family costim receptors (between CD20~0.02 and HER2~0.17/day)
sources.kdeg_perday: class-estimate: no molecule-specific free-receptor turnover published; default for inducible costim receptor (t1/2 ~1.4 day)
sources.mw_kda: class-estimate: full-length IgG standard MW 145-150 kDa (no molecule-specific sequence MW retrieved)
sources.route: Irenaeus S et al., Invest New Drugs 2022 (IV); OPTIMIZE-1 (NCT04888312) IV
sources.half_life_days: Moreno V et al., Invest New Drugs 2023;41:93-104. DOI 10.1007/s10637-022-01319-2; PMID 36538259. Full text retrieved: 'mitazalimab was rapidly cleared from the systemic circulation with mean terminal half-life of 11.9 and 24.1 h, respectively. Pharmacokinetics appeared to exhibit target-mediated drug disposition.' Midpoint 0.75 d (range 0.50-1.00 d).

mitazalimab.notes: Placeholder KD=1.0 REPLACED with 0.01 nM (10 pM, Mangsbo 2015). Mitazalimab = ADC-1013 / JNJ-64457107, affinity-matured fully human IgG1, very fast on-rate, FcgR-crosslink-dependent agonism. Half-life 0.75 d = midpoint of dose-dependent 0.50-1.00 d (TMDD). Originally developed for intratumoral dosing; now IV.

measured_vs_derived.KD_nM: measured (SPR/Biacore, human ICOS; from retrieved GSK clinical-protocol PDFs)
measured_vs_derived.kon_perM_pers: not extracted (ka/kd in patent Table 9; numeric not retrieved)

sources.KD_nM: GSK clinical protocols, PDFs retrieved: NCT03447314 and INDUCE-1 NCT02723955 both state 'GSK3359609 (feladilimab) bound to recombinant human ICOS with an affinity of 1.34 nM as determined by Biacore methodology.' NCT02723955 adds: 'binds with sub nanomolar affinity to human ICOS and cross-reacts with cynomolgus monkey ICOS (1.34 and 0.95 nM, respectively).'
sources.kint_perday: class-estimate: no molecule-specific internalization rate published for this costim receptor + agonist mAb; order-of-magnitude default for TNFRSF/CD28-family costim receptors (between CD20~0.02 and HER2~0.17/day)
sources.kdeg_perday: class-estimate: no molecule-specific free-receptor turnover published; default for inducible costim receptor (t1/2 ~1.4 day)
sources.mw_kda: class-estimate: full-length IgG standard MW 145-150 kDa (no molecule-specific sequence MW retrieved)
sources.fFcRn: isotype IgG4PE (S228P/F234A/L235E): reduced FcgR/C1q but FcRn retained; recycling convention 0.89
sources.half_life_days: no clean numeric terminal t1/2 found in accessible sources

feladilimab.notes: Placeholder KD=1.0 REPLACED with 1.34 nM (GSK Biacore). Feladilimab = GSK3359609 / H2L5, humanized IgG4PE; PE mutations attenuate activating FcgR/C1q but retain FcgRIIb (for agonism) and FcRn (for recycling). Not a superagonist (requires TCR engagement). Half-life null (not found).

measured_vs_derived.KD_nM: measured (BLI, ForteBio Octet, 1:1 global fit, human ICOS)
measured_vs_derived.kon_perM_pers: not published (Octet 1:1 fit; kon/koff not reported numerically)

sources.KD_nM: Hanson A et al., PLoS One 2020;15(9):e0239595. DOI 10.1371/journal.pone.0239595; PMID 32970735. Full text retrieved and read: 'a humanized IgG1 version called JTX-2011 was generated. JTX-2011 retained species cross-reactivity with affinities of 0.93 nM to hICOS, 0.46 nM to cynomolgus ICOS...' by ForteBio Octet (BLI).

vopratelimab.notes: Placeholder KD=1.0 REPLACED with 0.93 nM (Hanson 2020, BLI/Octet). Vopratelimab = JTX-2011 / clone 37A10, humanized IgG1-kappa; requires Fc cross-linking (FcgRIIb) + prior T-cell priming for agonism; not a superagonist. Half-life null (not found).

measured_vs_derived.KD_nM: DERIVED from retrieved comparator study (Chen 2023, SPR): computed as ~8x weaker than BAT6026's 0.28 nM. Approximate.

sources.KD_nM: DERIVED from Chen X et al., Front Oncol 2023;13:1211759. DOI 10.3389/fonc.2023.1211759. Full text retrieved and read: 'The affinity of BT6026 [BAT6026] to OX40 was 0.28nM, approximately 8 times stronger than that of PF-04518600.' => PF-04518600 (ivuxolimab) KD ~= 0.28 x 8 ~= 2.2 nM (SPR comparator, in-house PF-04518600 per US 9,028,824 B2 sequences). No direct primary numeric KD for ivuxolimab is published in accessible sources; Pfizer protocols describe only 'high affinity' qualitatively.
sources.kint_perday: class-estimate: no molecule-specific internalization rate published for this costim receptor + agonist mAb; order-of-magnitude default for TNFRSF/CD28-family costim receptors (between CD20~0.02 and HER2~0.17/day)
sources.kdeg_perday: class-estimate: no molecule-specific free-receptor turnover published; default for inducible costim receptor (t1/2 ~1.4 day)
sources.mw_kda: class-estimate: full-length IgG standard MW 145-150 kDa (no molecule-specific sequence MW retrieved)
sources.route: Pfizer NCT02315066 / Diab A et al. Clin Cancer Res 2022;28:71 (IV)
sources.half_life_days: Pfizer clinical protocol NCT03390296 (Prot_SAP), PDF retrieved: 'the PK of PF-04518600 in humans is expected to have a CL of 1.55 mL/kg/day, a Vc of 31.3 mL/kg, and t1/2 of approximately 25 days.' Cyno toxicity studies showed terminal t1/2 ~60-70 h. Value 25 d = predicted human.

ivuxolimab.notes: Placeholder KD=1.0 REPLACED with ~2.2 nM. DERIVED/APPROXIMATE: no direct primary numeric KD for ivuxolimab in accessible sources; computed from retrieved head-to-head SPR comparator (Chen 2023: ivuxolimab ~8x weaker than BAT6026's 0.28 nM => ~2.2 nM). Treat as approximate. Ivuxolimab = PF-04518600 / PF-8600, fully human IgG2. Half-life 25 d = predicted human (per NCT03390296 protocol, from cyno allometry); cyno terminal t1/2 ~60-70 h. Also studied SC.
